property.text: In compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff (SG-019), each generated proof MUST be valid for its corresponding cell and coset. forall i: verify_cell_kzg_proof(commit(poly), i, cells[i], proofs[i]) == True.

reachability.notes: Generated proofs are published to P2P. If any proof is invalid for its cell, receiving validators reject the sidecar.

property.severity_justification: Invalid proof generation would cause all receivers to reject the sidecar, effectively DoSing the proposer's block data availability.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing a valid blob producing a cell-proof pair that fails verification.

property.text: The vanishing polynomial produced by vanishing_polynomialcoeff (SG-012) MUST evaluate to zero at every point in the input xs. forall x in xs: evaluate_polynomialcoeff(vanishing_poly, x) == 0. The result MUST have degree exactly len(xs) and leading coefficient 1 (monic).

reachability.notes: The vanishing polynomial is constructed from missing cell roots which are derived from attacker-controlled withholding patterns.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect vanishing polynomial leads to wrong polynomial division results in both proof generation and recovery.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing incorrect vanishing polynomial for specific missing cell patterns.

property.text: In construct_vanishing_polynomial (SG-022), at least one cell MUST be present (not all cells can be missing). The missing_cell_indices set MUST be a strict subset of [0, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB).

reachability.notes: If all cells are missing, the vanishing polynomial has no roots to construct from, or spans the entire domain.

property.severity_justification: Edge case that should be caught by the 50% availability threshold but must be independently verified.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing what happens when construct_vanishing_polynomial receives all cells as missing.

property.text: In construct_vanishing_polynomial (SG-022), the coefficient spacing by FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL correctly extends the short vanishing polynomial to the full extended evaluation domain. zero_poly_coeff[i * FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL] == short_zero_poly[i] and all other coefficients are zero.

reachability.notes: Incorrect coefficient placement produces a different polynomial from the intended vanishing polynomial.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect coefficient spacing causes recovery to reconstruct the wrong polynomial.

property.text: In recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023), the algebraic identity E(x) * Z(x) == P(x) * Z(x) over the FFT domain MUST hold. The element-wise multiplication ez_eval[i] = zero_poly_eval[i] * extended_evaluation[i] MUST correctly represent this product in the evaluation domain.

reachability.notes: The algebraic identity is the mathematical foundation of the recovery algorithm. If violated, recovery produces incorrect polynomials.

property.severity_justification: Violation of the core algebraic identity causes incorrect polynomial recovery, propagating invalid data.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating that the algebraic identity is violated for specific input patterns.

property.text: Data column sidecars received over P2P MUST be verified via verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch before acceptance. The batch verification MUST check all cells in the sidecar against their claimed KZG commitments. Forged sidecars with fabricated KZG commitments and cells MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Core security property: P2P peers are untrusted. Every sidecar must be cryptographically verified.

property.severity_justification: Accepting unverified sidecars allows an attacker to inject arbitrary data as if it were committed to by the block proposer.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing a forged sidecar that is accepted without proper verification.

property.text: When reconstructing sidecars from an existing sidecar via get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar (Validator SG-005), the input sidecar's signed_block_header, kzg_commitments, and kzg_commitments_inclusion_proof MUST have been verified against the canonical chain BEFORE calling this function. The function itself does NOT re-verify these fields.

reachability.notes: An attacker provides a sidecar with a forged block header. If the caller does not verify the header before calling this function, reconstructed sidecars are linked to a non-existent block.

property.severity_justification: Accepting sidecars with forged block headers causes the node to distribute data linked to non-existent or malicious blocks, corrupting the data availability layer.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing sidecar reconstruction from forged header that is then distributed to peers.

property.text: get_validators_custody_requirement (Validator SG-001) MUST always return a value in [VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS]. The clamping formula min(max(count, VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT), NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS) MUST be correctly applied.

reachability.notes: Internal computation. Incorrect custody requirement weakens data availability sampling guarantees.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect custody requirement causes validators to custody too few or too many groups, weakening network-level data availability guarantees.

property.text: BALANCE_PER_ADDITIONAL_CUSTODY_GROUP MUST be a non-zero protocol constant. The integer division total_node_balance // BALANCE_PER_ADDITIONAL_CUSTODY_GROUP in get_validators_custody_requirement (Validator SG-001) MUST NOT divide by zero.

reachability.notes: Protocol constant validation at startup. If set to zero (configuration error), division by zero crashes the node.

property.severity_justification: Division by zero in constant computation crashes the node at startup.

property.text: In cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction (Validator SG-002), blobs_bundle.proofs MUST have length exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs_bundle.blobs). The slicing indices [start:end] where start = i * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB and end = (i+1) * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB MUST always be in bounds.

reachability.notes: Proofs come from the EL via Engine API. If the EL is compromised and provides a shorter proofs array, slicing silently returns fewer proofs, producing mismatched cell-proof pairs.

property.severity_justification: Silently truncated proofs produce sidecars with mismatched cells and proofs, which would fail verification at receiving validators.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a compromised EL providing short proofs array that bypasses length assertion.

property.text: In get_data_column_sidecars (Validator SG-003), exactly NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS sidecars MUST be produced. Each sidecar's index field MUST equal the column_index. len(cells_and_kzg_proofs) MUST equal len(kzg_commitments).

reachability.notes: Incorrect number of sidecars or mismatched indices would cause receiving validators to reject sidecars or attribute data to wrong columns.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect sidecar count or index mismatch causes data availability sampling failures.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing sidecar index mismatch.

property.text: Each sidecar MUST be published to exactly one subnet determined by compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar(column_index) (Validator SG-006). The subnet_id MUST be deterministically computed from column_index. All sidecars MUST be published before completion.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could publish sidecars to wrong subnets. Receiving validators must verify sidecar.index matches the expected subnet.

property.severity_justification: Publishing to wrong subnets or selectively withholding sidecars undermines data availability, potentially preventing finality.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating selective withholding that prevents data availability sampling.

property.text: Data column sidecars MUST be retained for at least MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS epochs (Validator SG-007). During the retention period, sidecars MUST be served when requested. Pruning is only permitted AFTER the retention period expires.

reachability.notes: Premature pruning prevents peers from verifying data availability, potentially causing incorrect finality assessments.

property.severity_justification: Premature pruning affects data availability for peers performing sampling or recovery, but does not directly cause consensus failure.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing premature pruning causing data availability sampling failure.

property.text: The co-located EL communicates via JWT-authenticated Engine API (tb-004). Blob data from the EL SHOULD be verified by CL before entering the polynomial commitment pipeline. If EL data is used without verification, a compromised EL can inject corrupted blobs.

reachability.notes: The EL is semi-trusted (JWT authenticated) but a compromised EL could provide corrupted blob data that enters the PeerDAS pipeline.

property.severity_justification: Requires EL compromise (JWT bypass or EL vulnerability). Impact is corrupted blob data entering the DA pipeline.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating EL compromise scenario and corrupted blob propagation.

property.text: A block proposer that withholds more than 50% of data columns MUST NOT have the block finalized. PeerDAS's 50% Reed-Solomon redundancy allows recovery from up to 50% missing columns, but beyond that threshold, recovery is mathematically impossible.

reachability.notes: Selective data withholding by a malicious proposer. Validators performing DAS must detect unavailability and not attest to the block.

property.severity_justification: If a block with >50% withheld columns is finalized, the data is irrecoverably lost, breaking the data availability guarantee.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating a scenario where >50% withholding is not detected and block is finalized.

property.text: The batch KZG verification in verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (SG-015) MUST limit computational cost. Implementations SHOULD apply cheap pre-checks (length validation, range checks on cell_indices) BEFORE expensive cryptographic operations (g1_lincomb, interpolation, pairing).

reachability.notes: An attacker floods the validator with invalid sidecars that require full cryptographic verification before rejection. Pre-checks reduce the attack surface.

property.severity_justification: CPU exhaustion via expensive cryptographic operations on invalid input. Mitigated by P2P rate limiting but could still degrade performance.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating significant CPU impact from crafted invalid sidecars.

property.text: The recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023) function has computational complexity involving multiple FFT operations (O(n log n)) and vanishing polynomial construction (O(k^2) where k is missing cells). An attacker MUST NOT be able to trigger unbounded computation via controlled missing cell patterns.

reachability.notes: The maximum number of missing cells is bounded by CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2 (the 50% threshold). Computational cost is bounded but could be significant.

property.severity_justification: Bounded but potentially significant computational cost during recovery. The 50% threshold limits the maximum number of missing cells.

property.text: Timing side channels in KZG batch verification MUST NOT leak information about which commitments are cached, which cell_indices are locally available, or the internal verification batch structure. Cryptographic field operations SHOULD be constant-time.

reachability.notes: Timing attacks require precise measurement and are generally difficult to exploit remotely over P2P.

property.severity_justification: Information disclosure via timing channels. Limited exploitability over noisy P2P networks.

property.text: A malicious block proposer MUST NOT be able to modify data column sidecar contents after computing valid cells and proofs. Receiving validators MUST independently verify each sidecar via verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch and verify the kzg_commitments_inclusion_proof against the block's Merkle root.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer swaps cells between columns or provides mismatched column_index and data. Independent verification by receivers must detect this.

property.severity_justification: Modified sidecars with swapped cells would corrupt data availability if accepted by validators.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing a modified sidecar (swapped cells) that bypasses verification.

property.text: A block proposer that delays publishing sidecars to some subnets (delayed withholding) within the attestation window MUST NOT cause validators to make incorrect finality assessments. Fork choice rules MUST account for delayed data availability.

reachability.notes: Strategic delay within the attestation window is hard to distinguish from network latency. Validators on delayed subnets may fail DAS and not attest.

property.severity_justification: Delayed withholding can cause reorgs or delayed finality but is partially mitigated by Reed-Solomon redundancy.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating delayed withholding causing finality delays.

property.text: The O(n^2) complexity of compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff (SG-019) MUST NOT cause unacceptable delays during block processing. Implementations SHOULD use the FK20 algorithm for O(n log n) complexity. With MAX_BLOBS_PER_BLOCK blobs, the total computation MUST complete within the block processing time budget.

reachability.notes: An attacker submits many blobs per block to maximize computational cost. The O(n^2) reference implementation may be vulnerable during high blob throughput.

property.severity_justification: Computational DoS via high blob count. Bounded by MAX_BLOBS_PER_BLOCK but O(n^2) implementations may still struggle.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating block processing timeout from maximum blob count with O(n^2) implementation.

property.text: The construct_vanishing_polynomial (SG-022) computational cost is O(k^2) where k is the number of missing cells. With the maximum of CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2 missing cells, the cost MUST be bounded and MUST NOT cause recovery to exceed reasonable time limits.

reachability.notes: An attacker withholds specific columns to maximize the number of missing cells at the 50% boundary, maximizing vanishing polynomial construction cost.

property.severity_justification: Bounded computational cost but could be significant at the 50% boundary. Specific to implementations using iterative polynomial multiplication.

property.text: Excessive data column sidecar requests during the retention period MUST NOT exhaust storage I/O or network bandwidth. Rate limiting per peer SHOULD be implemented. The retention period is bounded by MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS.

reachability.notes: An attacker floods a validator with sidecar requests. Rate limiting is recommended but not mandated by the specification.

property.severity_justification: Resource exhaustion via excessive requests. Mitigated by P2P rate limiting and bounded retention period.

property.text: In polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007), the input polynomial MUST have exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB evaluations. The bit-reversal permutation MUST be applied BEFORE the inverse FFT.

reachability.notes: The input comes from blob_to_polynomial which processes attacker-submitted blob data.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect evaluation count produces wrong polynomial coefficients, corrupting all downstream operations.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing incorrect coefficients from wrong evaluation count.

property.text: In compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (SG-014), the quotient polynomial Q(X) = f(X) / Z(X) MUST satisfy the KZG proof relationship: the proof is a commitment to Q(X), and the verification equation e(proof, [s^n]) == e(commit(f) - commit(I), [1]) holds for interpolation polynomial I.

reachability.notes: Core KZG proof generation correctness. If violated, generated proofs would fail verification.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect proof generation means all cells from this node fail verification, effectively DoSing the proposer.

property.text: evaluate_polynomialcoeff (SG-013) MUST correctly implement Horner's method: for polynomial p(X) = sum(coeff[i] * X^i), evaluate_polynomialcoeff(coeff, z) MUST return p(z). The evaluation MUST process coefficients in reverse order.

reachability.notes: Fundamental arithmetic building block. Incorrect evaluation affects proof generation and cell computation.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect polynomial evaluation corrupts proof generation and cell computation throughout the pipeline.

property.text: In get_data_column_sidecars_from_block (Validator SG-004), the signed_block_header MUST be correctly computed from the signed_block, and the kzg_commitments_inclusion_proof MUST be a valid Merkle proof over the blob_kzg_commitments field in the block body.

reachability.notes: Incorrect Merkle proof computation would cause receiving validators to reject all sidecars from this proposer.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect Merkle proof causes all sidecars to be rejected, preventing data availability for the block.

property.text: In compute_cells (SG-018), each cell MUST represent polynomial evaluations over a distinct coset. The output MUST contain exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells, and cell[i] MUST correspond to evaluations over coset_for_cell(i).

reachability.notes: Incorrect cell-coset correspondence causes verification failures and incorrect recovery.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect cell-coset mapping produces cells that fail verification or cause incorrect recovery.

property.text: In coset_for_cell (SG-017), the coset elements MUST form a coset h*G = {h, h*g, h*g^2, ...} where h = coset_shift_for_cell(cell_index) and g is a generator of the subgroup of order FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL.

reachability.notes: Incorrect coset structure causes verification equation to fail for valid data.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect coset structure breaks all verification and recovery operations.

property.text: In add_polynomialcoeff (SG-008), the result degree MUST equal max(deg(a), deg(b)). The shorter polynomial MUST be correctly zero-padded for missing coefficients during addition.

reachability.notes: Polynomial addition is used extensively in interpolation and recovery. Incorrect addition corrupts all downstream results.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect polynomial addition corrupts interpolation and recovery results.

property.text: In multiply_polynomialcoeff (SG-009), the result degree MUST equal deg(a) + deg(b). The convolution of coefficients MUST be correctly computed.

reachability.notes: Polynomial multiplication is used in vanishing polynomial construction and interpolation.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect polynomial multiplication corrupts vanishing polynomial construction and interpolation.

property.text: In divide_polynomialcoeff (SG-010), the quotient and remainder MUST satisfy a = quotient * b + remainder. The remainder is discarded, which is correct only when the division is exact (remainder is zero).

reachability.notes: Division is used in compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl. If the division is not exact, the discarded remainder introduces errors.

property.severity_justification: Non-exact division produces incorrect quotient polynomial, corrupting KZG proofs.

property.text: In interpolate_polynomialcoeff (SG-011), the result polynomial MUST have degree at most len(xs) - 1, and MUST satisfy evaluate(result, xs[i]) == ys[i] for all i. This is the Lagrange interpolation correctness property.

reachability.notes: Interpolation is used in the batch verification equation. Incorrect interpolation causes the verification to produce wrong results.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect interpolation in the verification equation could cause valid proofs to be rejected or invalid proofs to be accepted.

property.text: In coset_fft_field (SG-005), the forward coset FFT MUST shift values by shift_factor before applying FFT, and the inverse coset FFT MUST apply inverse FFT then unshift by shift_factor.inverse(). The composition forward(inverse(x)) MUST equal x.

reachability.notes: Coset FFT is critical for the recovery algorithm in recover_polynomialcoeff.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect coset FFT ordering breaks the recovery algorithm, producing wrong polynomials.

property.text: encode_receipt MUST produce a receipt encoding whose first byte is exactly the type prefix corresponding to the transaction type: 0x01 for AccessListTransaction, 0x02 for FeeMarketTransaction, 0x03 for BlobTransaction, 0x04 for SetCodeTransaction. For LegacyTransaction, the receipt MUST be returned without any type prefix (raw RLP).

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit transactions of any supported type via P2P. Incorrect encoding causes consensus splits between clients that encode differently.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect receipt encoding produces a divergent receipts_root in the block header, causing a consensus split across the network. All clients must agree on encoding.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating that a specific transaction type produces a receipt encoding that differs from the canonical specification.

property.text: encode_receipt MUST handle ALL transaction types exhaustively. If a new transaction type is introduced without updating encode_receipt, the function MUST NOT silently fall through to the legacy encoding path. The set {AccessListTransaction, FeeMarketTransaction, BlobTransaction, SetCodeTransaction, LegacyTransaction} MUST be exhaustive.

reachability.notes: If a future transaction type is added without updating encode_receipt, receipts for that type would be silently encoded as legacy, producing incorrect encodings that cause consensus splits.

property.severity_justification: Silent fall-through to legacy encoding for an unrecognized transaction type corrupts the receipts_root and causes consensus failure.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstration of a transaction type that is not handled by the type-dispatch in encode_receipt.

property.text: LegacyTransaction receipts MUST be returned as raw Receipt objects without RLP wrapping or type-prefix prepending. forall (tx : LegacyTransaction, receipt : Receipt): encode_receipt(tx, receipt) == receipt.

reachability.notes: Attacker can submit legacy transactions via P2P. Wrapping a legacy receipt would alter the receipts_root.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect encoding of legacy receipts would cause receipt root mismatch and block rejection or consensus split.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: submit a legacy transaction and demonstrate receipt encoding differs from raw Receipt object.

property.text: decode_receipt MUST assert that if the receipt is of type Bytes, the first byte MUST be in the set {1, 2, 3, 4}. Any other first byte value MUST cause an assertion failure. forall (receipt : Bytes): receipt[0] not in {1, 2, 3, 4} => decode_receipt(receipt) reverts.

reachability.notes: A malicious peer could send a receipt with an invalid type-prefix byte via devp2p. The assertion is the primary gatekeeper.

property.severity_justification: Bypassing the type-prefix assertion could cause the decoder to accept invalid receipts, leading to state corruption or consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a receipt with type-prefix byte outside {1,2,3,4} and demonstrate it passes decode_receipt without assertion failure.

property.text: decode_receipt MUST strip exactly the first byte (type prefix) before RLP decoding the remainder. forall (receipt : Bytes, receipt[0] in {1,2,3,4}): decode_receipt(receipt) == rlp.decode_to(Receipt, receipt[1:]).

reachability.notes: Incorrect offset in byte stripping would cause RLP decoding to fail or produce corrupt receipt data.

property.severity_justification: Off-by-one in prefix stripping corrupts all receipt decoding, causing consensus splits between clients that decode differently.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that decode_receipt strips an incorrect number of bytes before RLP decoding.

property.text: encode_receipt and decode_receipt MUST be inverse operations for all typed transactions: forall (tx, receipt): decode_receipt(encode_receipt(tx, receipt)) == receipt.

reachability.notes: If encode and decode are not exact inverses, data transmitted between peers via P2P will be corrupted, causing network partitions.

property.severity_justification: Non-invertible encoding/decoding causes data corruption across the P2P network and consensus splits.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a receipt where encode followed by decode produces a different receipt object.

property.text: The Withdrawal data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). All four fields (index: U64, validator_index: U64, address: Address, amount: U256) MUST be set before the freeze operation.

reachability.notes: Withdrawals originate from the consensus layer via Engine API. A compromised CL could send malformed withdrawal instructions.

property.severity_justification: Mutable withdrawal objects could be tampered with after construction, leading to unauthorized ETH credits.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate mutation of a Withdrawal object after freeze, showing unauthorized balance modification.

property.text: All Withdrawal fields MUST be set during construction. A Withdrawal with any unset field MUST NOT be freezable. forall (w : Withdrawal): freeze(w) requires w.index != None AND w.validator_index != None AND w.address != None AND w.amount != None.

reachability.notes: Partial withdrawal construction could lead to undefined behavior when serialized or processed.

property.severity_justification: Partially constructed withdrawals could cause undefined behavior or crashes during serialization.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Withdrawal with a missing field can be frozen and used in block processing.

property.text: The Header data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). All 21 fields MUST be set in the specified construction order before freeze.

reachability.notes: Block headers arrive via P2P from peers. A mutable header could be tampered with after initial validation.

property.severity_justification: A mutable header object could be modified after validation, bypassing consensus checks and allowing invalid state transitions.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Header field can be mutated after freeze, allowing post-validation tampering.

property.text: In post-merge (PoS) forks, the Header difficulty field MUST be 0 and the nonce field MUST be zero-bytes. forall (header : Header, post_merge): header.difficulty == 0 AND header.nonce == Bytes8(0).

reachability.notes: A peer could transmit a block with non-zero difficulty or nonce in post-merge context, violating PoS constraints.

property.severity_justification: Accepting a block with non-zero difficulty/nonce in PoS could indicate a consensus rule violation, allowing invalid blocks.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: craft a post-merge block with non-zero difficulty and demonstrate it passes header validation.

property.text: In post-merge forks, the ommers_hash in the Header MUST always equal EMPTY_OMMER_HASH. forall (header : Header, post_merge): header.ommers_hash == EMPTY_OMMER_HASH.

reachability.notes: A malicious peer could submit a block with non-empty ommers_hash to exploit clients that don't validate this field.

property.severity_justification: Accepting blocks with non-empty ommers in PoS would break protocol invariants and could enable uncle reward exploitation.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct a post-merge block with ommers_hash != EMPTY_OMMER_HASH and show it passes validation.

property.text: The base_fee_per_gas in the Header MUST follow EIP-1559 dynamic fee adjustment rules relative to the parent block's base fee and gas usage.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could set an incorrect base_fee_per_gas to undercharge for block space.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect base fee allows blocks with mispriced transactions, undermining the EIP-1559 fee market and potentially enabling spam attacks.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block with incorrect base_fee_per_gas that passes validation.

property.text: The blob_gas_used and excess_blob_gas fields in the Header MUST follow EIP-4844 blob gas accounting rules.

reachability.notes: Incorrect blob gas accounting allows blob spam or underpriced blob transactions.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect blob gas accounting breaks the EIP-4844 fee market for data availability and could enable underpriced blob spam.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block with incorrect blob_gas_used or excess_blob_gas that passes validation.

property.text: The requests_hash field in the Header MUST follow EIP-7685 general purpose request computation. forall (header): header.requests_hash == compute_requests_hash(requests).

reachability.notes: Incorrect requests_hash causes CL-EL disagreement and block rejection at consensus level.

property.severity_justification: requests_hash mismatch between CL and EL causes consensus failure, preventing block finalization.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate a block where requests_hash does not match the computed value from the actual requests.

property.text: The Block data structure MUST be immutable after construction (slotted_freezable). A Block consists of (header: Header, transactions: Tuple[Bytes|LegacyTransaction, ...], ommers: Tuple[Header, ...], withdrawals: Tuple[Withdrawal, ...]).

reachability.notes: Blocks arrive via P2P. Mutability after construction could allow post-validation tampering.

property.severity_justification: Mutable block objects could be tampered with between validation and execution, allowing invalid state transitions.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: demonstrate that a Block object can be mutated after construction and freeze.

property.text: In post-merge (PoS) forks, the ommers tuple in a Block MUST always be empty. forall (block : Block, post_merge): len(block.ommers) == 0.

reachability.notes: A peer could transmit a block with non-empty ommers to exploit clients that don't validate this post-merge.

property.severity_justification: Accepting blocks with ommers in PoS violates protocol invariants and could enable reward manipulation.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: construct a post-merge block with non-empty ommers and demonstrate it passes validation.
